_comment: Reference example showing the complete audit_data.json shape build_audit.js expects. Built from the ClearanceLab pilot run. Use this as a template — every section is optional except client.name. Save your real audit data as audit_data.json, then run: node build_audit.js audit_data.json output.docx

client.name: Clearance Lab
client.subtitle: Positioning, Pricing & Feature Gaps in AI Label-Compliance Tooling

executiveSummary.paragraph: Clearance Lab is a credit-priced, AI-native label compliance checker for FDA, EU FIC, AAFCO, and adjacent frameworks across food, pet, cosmetics, supplements, and pharma. It enters a category that has split into three camps: AI-native MVPs (the client's tier), AI-augmented enterprise platforms, and incumbent regulatory/supplier suites.
bullets: Pricing is genuinely disruptive. The client sells scans for $0.83–$1.27. The closest AI competitor starts at $500/month. At any volume below ~400 scans/month, the client is 5–50× cheaper.
Multi-industry breadth is unique. No competitor covers all five domains at this price point.
Trust and depth are the real gaps. No SOC 2, no named regulatory advisor, no enterprise tier.
executiveSummary.callout: Bottom line: the product is priced for self-serve and SMB. The upside is opening a credible enterprise track ($25K–$60K/yr) without breaking the self-serve magic.

items.head: Tagline:
items.body: "Ensure Regulatory Compliance with AI." Upload packaging → instant compliance analysis.

items.head: Core promise:
items.body: AI-powered review in under 60 seconds, with prioritized fixes.

items.head: Output:
items.body: Severity-ranked report with specific regulation citations and exportable PDF.

items.head: Human food:
items.body: FDA 21 CFR · EU FIC 1169/2011 · FSA · CFIA

clientSnapshot.pricingIntro: Credit-based, no subscription. Unique in the category.

rows: All domains, fix recs, PDF, email support
+ audit trail, team seats, priority support

callouts: Footer reads "© 2026 Capsule" — fix brand mismatch before any enterprise prospect notices.
flags: No enterprise tier above $249 — leaves $25K+ deals on the table.
No SOC 2 badge or security page surface.

landscape.intro: Four tiers organize the AI label-compliance category by pricing posture and product depth.

facts.label: Pitch
facts.value: Instant FDA compliance check; 1–100 score in under 30 seconds.

facts.label: Threat level
facts.value: HIGH — closest direct competitor with public pricing

pricing.intro: Pricing in the category spans roughly two orders of magnitude.
commentary: Lead with the price gap above the fold on the site.
Add a Pro Monthly subscription tier as a hedge against credit-pack churn.

features.intro: Y = supports today, ~ = partial/unclear, N = not in scope.

wins: Pricing & access. $19 entry, $0.83–$1.27 per scan — nothing else in the AI tier comes close.
Multi-industry breadth. Five domains in one workspace.
parity: FDA, EU FIC, AAFCO framework coverage — every competitor advertises these.
gaps: No SOC 2 / SSO / enterprise security signals.

recommendations.intro: Ordered by leverage — impact per unit of effort.
a: Add a Trust / Security page (SOC 2 status, encryption, data residency).
Add a named regulatory advisor to the site.
Lead with the price comparison above the fold.
b: Public regulation coverage matrix (CFR Parts, EU regs, version-dated).
c: Publish a self-serve Starter tier ($199–$299/mo, 25 label reviews/mo).
Add a Pro Monthly subscription alongside credit packs.
d: Lean into the "only AI tool that covers all five domains" wedge.

openQuestions.intro: Answers to these will sharpen the next pass.
items: What is the current pricing and packaging? (Tiers, what's gated where.)
Who is the primary ICP today?
Which industry has the most paying customers right now?
